mappings.rationale: Section B.1 specifically covers dictionaries and lexicons as systems for storage and retrieval of information.
Section B covers storage and retrieval of information including databases, electronic publishing, and the principles underlying information systems.
Section B.6 covers bibliographic and numeric databases, and big data extends techniques of information storage and retrieval.
Sections B.6-7 cover databases and electronic storage, and C.3.b covers electronic publishing, all relying on computer science.
Electronic systems for information storage, retrieval, and dissemination (B.6-7, C.3.b) depend on computing technology.
The evolution of computing technology transformed the institutions and techniques for information management in this section.
Cryptography relates to secure storage and transmission of information discussed in section B.
Writing is the foundational technology for information storage and retrieval, underlying the systems in B and C.
Reading is the essential complement to publishing (C.3) and libraries (B.4) as means of knowledge dissemination.
Hieroglyphs represent an ancient system of writing and information preservation foundational to the techniques in this section.
Section C.3.a.ii covers newspapers and C.3.a.iii covers periodicals as forms of print publishing for knowledge dissemination.
Typography is central to print publishing (C.3.a), the selection, preparation, and marketing of printed material.
Photography is a technique for preservation and documentation relevant to museums (A.1), archives (B.5), and publishing (C.3).
Section C.4 covers broadcasting including television as a medium for knowledge dissemination.
Section B.7 covers magnetic and optical recordings, and C.4 covers broadcasting, both central to audio knowledge dissemination.
Cinema is a medium for knowledge dissemination related to broadcasting (C.4) and electronic publishing (C.3.b).
Indian cinema relates to broadcasting (C.4), printing, and radio as institutions for knowledge dissemination.
Section C.1 references educational institutions for the advancement and dissemination of knowledge.
Section C.1 references educational institutions as key institutions for the advancement and dissemination of knowledge.
Section C.5 covers observatories and planetariums as institutions for the advancement of astronomical knowledge.
Section C.5 covers observatories and planetariums, which rely on telescopes as primary instruments.
Section A.1 covers museums including museums of history and natural history where archaeological collections are preserved.
Section A.1.b.v covers museums of art and art galleries as institutions for protecting and storing artistic objects.
Section A.1.b.v covers art museums and galleries, and contemporary art raises questions about institutional collection practices.
Section A.1.b.v covers museums of art and art galleries including the Museum of Modern Art.

mappings.vsiAuthor: John A. Matthews, David T. Herbert
mappings.rationale: Section B.3 covers atlases and map collections as systems for storage and retrieval of geographic information.
Section A.5 covers parks and nature preserves for the preservation of biological knowledge and collections.
Sections A.4 and A.5 cover zoological gardens, botanical gardens, and nature preserves for ecological knowledge.
Intellectual property law governs knowledge dissemination through publishing (C.3) and electronic media (C.3.b, C.4).
Surveillance technologies relate to the collection, storage, and retrieval of information in B.
Section C.2 covers academies and societies for the advancement of knowledge, which drive innovation.
Oral history involves recording, archiving, and preserving knowledge through interviews, relevant to B.5 (archives) and B.7 (recordings).
Sections A.4.c and A.5 cover botanical gardens and arboretums as institutions for preserving botanical knowledge.
Section B.3 covers atlases and map collections, and navigation instruments relate to the preservation of geographic knowledge.
